channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 30 replies — 95.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-27 01:28 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417516
Bullish.

62417517
>>62417511
Brad gargleballs is an even bigger scammer.

62417535
>>62417503
>Chainlink Labs is transitioning

62417540
>>62417517
All the major institutions offering xrp ETF’s don’t seem to think so

62417561
>>62417503
makes sense

62417568
>>62417540
Theyre selling a product and making money from it. Thats it.

62417574
The only actual FUD I’ve felt about Link in 8 years was when they brought in this BUILD program. I was never interested in any sort of incentives outside of the coin, and i had zero interest in staking. I was always of the mindset that Link will go to $2,000 per coin or whatever; what the fuck do I care about getting some asshole’s project coins and increasing my net worth by 2% with it mooning. Even staking rewards I don’t give a fuck about. Although in hindsight I probably should have staked to get the extra 10% in free coins over the last few years, but I would have unstaked and sold irregardless once the price of Link got to $500 or whatever

62417587
>>62417514
More like Chainlink is stealing your rewards and giving it to themselves.

62417599
>>62417568
Hahaha, exactly

62417733
The main reason this should sound an alarm for linkies is that there's no reason they couldn't say "actually we're shutting down staking" or "actually we're shutting down need for the $LINK token" at any time. What's actually stopping them from doing this?

62417778
>>62417733
This is why I never staked. Staking came after the FTX scam. No fucking way I’m not having possession of my coins. For what? 4 measly % in rewards. You dumb motherfuckers and your staking rewards. It doesn’t fucking matter if the coin is at $5,000 and you get staking rewards cuz you are already filthy rich. I hope they seize all your shit and I’ll be at the yacht party alone with with a bunch of bitches and some /biz/ jannies

62417782
>>62417778
to play devil's advocate the one way it pays is if the pool legitimately never really opens back up and being a staker winds up being extraordinarily profitable.

62417793
>>62417782
I guarantee you that within 2 years all APY coming from staking will go to reserve. The writing is on the wall and it couldn't be anymore obvious.

Where is your GMX fee share? Link marines were promised 1.2%

You're still on staking v0.2 after being promised rapid, feature heavy updates for 4 years.

Now the team is telling you they are going to pocket the fees the COMMUNITY was subsidizing through BUILD.

I honestly think Sergey has a death wish.

62417802
>>62417793
>Where is your GMX fee share?
buried by Hyperliquid

62417814
>>62417599
an ETF isnt an endorsement of the company itself lol

62417833
>>62417793
I'm not arguing the reality, the only think linkies have to hold onto is the possibility of a better future at this point. I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

62417836
>>62417802
GMX fee share is going to the reserve, actually.

62417855
They’re switching it to Link tokens. Nice.

62417873
>>62417782
It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year… Is life good? Absolutely. But that’s at the expense of having 20 million locked up that you can’t touch. Personally to me that’s boring as fuck and it’s too much money coming in a year where making any more money matters. You’ll probably fall into bad devices unless you have an extreme passion for something.

I’ll take the 20 mil. And wing it and save 1,000 link buried somewhere in case I fuck up. And by then link might be 10x more then what it initially was when you first sold so you’ll probably have an equal amount of money if you fuck up.

62417888
>>62417782
I think the ONLY people who should stake are in the 1,000 to 3,000 stack range.

3,000 link at $1,000 is 3 million. You have no room for error here. You have about 90k coming in yearly post tax. This is great. You can NEET it up and be very aggressive in investing in your passion or whatever which will give you purpose and try to bring that 3 million up to 10 million or whatever and you’ll feel a sense of pride. But if you get clobbered investing say 60k 1 year…. It doesn’t matter cuz you got your 90k per year social security payment coming in yearly

62417901
>>62417503
Thats good

Literally every build project was useless shitcoin

62417921
>>62417836
yeah, just saying it's a pittance since HL disrupted the entire perps market simply by turning their token-permissioned validator set into the oracle network

62418149
>>62417888
until they turn around and say pools closed.
It's looking highly likely that staking will only be a thing for the 45M tokens already staked.
Staking is a completed system that doesn't require any further updates or any more people involved/staking

62418503
>chainlink reserve announced
>treated by everyone as a mild gesture of good will to holders to make up for the regular market dumps
>there's no system or contract actually locking LINK into the reserve, they could dump it all too if they chose to
>doesn't matter though, it's a sideshow compared to the value staking 1.0 would bring
>suddenly staking 1.0 goes from "2 more weeks" vaporware to MIA in any blog posts or q&a's
>not even brought up with a "in the event of Clarity Act passing..." theoretical, it's simply gone
>instead, twitter PR and announcement posts start consistently pushing the reserve as the main source of future LINK value
>despite the fact that it's still a smalltime sideshow that doesn't actually lock or burn anything
I'm starting to experience feelings of fear, uncertainty, and even doubt about my investment

62418519
being in the usa i couldn't get those rewards anyway
and no i didn't want to risk anything with a VPN

62418676
>>62417503
after the claim shitshow its not surprising
i didnt even bother claiming those shitcoins

62418693
>>62418149
Who cares? Dont get me wrong I see the point of staking in theory… plug your 10k link in there and make 500k/year or whatever. My point is it doesn’t matter if you take. Cuz you got 10 million whether you stake or not

item.title: GET THE FUCK OUT OF AI STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW THE BUBBLE IS ABOUT TO BURST SO FUCKING HARD THANK YOU SAM ALTMAN SELL E… — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
GET THE FUCK OUT OF AI STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW THE BUBBLE IS ABOUT TO BURST SO FUCKING HARD THANK YOU SAM ALTMAN SELL E…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-27 01:41 UTC

OP

GET THE FUCK OUT OF AI STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW THE BUBBLE IS ABOUT TO BURST SO FUCKING HARD THANK YOU SAM ALTMAN SELL EVERYTHING RIGHT NOW

Early replies (first 3)

62417878
>>62417864
SanDisk ISNT worth $300B, y-you be sayin?

62417885
is the great crypto rotation finally here

62417890
>>62417864
NAH NIGGER, I'll be buying every DIP.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417916
m'droolrag, lad?

62417923
>up 30% in a month
>down 10%
FREAK THE FUCK OUT AND SELL SELL SELL

62417931
>>62417923
SELL NOW OR SELL AFTER ANOTHER -30% I DONT CARE
>>62417885
YES NIGGER

62417958
>>62417923
man, I remember hearing the same about btc 8 months ago

62417985
And switch to what? Retail? Healthcare? Like it or not AI stock is the only industry showing any type of growth right now.

62417990
>>62417985
what a negative IQ does to a mf

62418000
>>62417985
LLY

62418018
>>62417990
THIS
>>62417985
DIGITAL SILVER AKA ETHEREUM
>>62417985
>AI stock is the only industry showing any type of growth right now.
literallly not true btw you are exit liquidity if you think this

62418028
>buy 500k worth of IREN shares at $47
>sell at $100
>you are now a millionaire
convince me not to

62418325
>>62417864
Yep. Buy DOT

62418458
>>62417923
Yeah it'd be fine if we weren't in an energy crisis. Buy the dip anon, don't slurp gulp that shit. Whatever you do, don't think about why gold is also crashing and the implication. AI is the future and everyone will have money to pay for it.

62418462
>>62417885
lol, lmao

62418567
>>62418458
>if we weren't in an energy crisis
there is no energy crisis wtf are you talking about?

62418689
>>62418028
you're just funding their $6B ATM

but incinerate your money if you want to, your choice

62418718
Get out means I will lose money. Ok I will short it then. Thanks for the signal.

item.title: >BTC falls another 5% in five days How does it feel to be the greater fool? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>BTC falls another 5% in five days How does it feel to be the greater fool?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-27 01:44 UTC

OP

>BTC falls another 5% in five days

How does it feel to be the greater fool?

Early replies (first 3)

62418381
>>62418307
Where were you in 2022? And 2018? And 2014?

This was inevitable, we also have further to go, bottom is 35k. You'll also sit back and watch bitcoin go to 250k without ever putting money in because you're gay.

62418475
>>62418381
It's over, anon. BTC boomed when it was the only game in town. Now it has to compete with a zillion other cryptos, AI, and space. No one is buying your bag.

62418480
>>62418475
Very bullish

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62418715
>>62418307
Reminder that Bitcoin is an IQ test.

62418723
>>62418475
Ok faggot, you were saying the same shit in 2022, then bitcoin broke $100k in 2024.

item.title: This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in cry… — 25 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in cry…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-27 00:30 UTC

OP

This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in crypto. Not a single goddamn one. ZERO exceptions

Early replies (first 3)

62410760
i mean he looks like a sojack, thats kind of on you

62413165
This, combined with fallout from the LINK AMA, is funny as hell. Assblaster was a fraud

62413241
>>62410743
Give context you nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413259
>>62413241
Yes, who is the onion and why do I care?>>62413165
>Assblaster was a fraud
This was always obvious

62413262
>>62413241
https://www.coinmetro.com/blog/announcement-onboarding-depositing-and-withdrawals
There are people in their telegram group talking about roping as a result of this. I'm not joking

62413573
>>62413262
So another exchange shutting down and stealing all users funds?

62414199
>>62413262
The ama was hard to watch. The amount of times he had to say he can't guarantee people's funds and that it's up to the courts was irritating. He kept saying they are all in it together and that he was too as in investor, ceo and user of the platform. I bet he didn't halt his own withdrawals though. What a fuckin goon on the whole "reorganization" pitch. Hopefully karma gets that stupid mother fucker. His body language said it all.

62414273
it's over. the sec need to be restored so it can go after every single piece of shit crypto project and exchange

62414281
>>62410743
>what if we had banks but like there we no rules about how the bankers behaved
Truly amazing how anyone thought this wasn't a scam.

62414293
>>62414281
Libertardian dream

62414317
>>62414199
>>62413262
Not your keys, not your crypto. Does remind me of last cycle when Kucoin closed up though. Hmm…

62414659
>>62410743

62414772
imagine trusting a man with a hairline like that? couldnt be me

62414835
>>62410743
My 1 whole btc is on there. I procrastinated putting it my own wallet. Are they scaming or we can get it back?

62414898
How the fuck does an exchange go bankrupt without a huge hack? Its like Trump bankrupting a casino. It should not happen

62414931
>>62410743
tfw a personal friend of mine for years started a crypto coin that actually took off when musk mentioned it a few years ago. it mooned and he sold all he held to pay off his house. black lives matter-tier scammer. you literally cannot trust anyone.

62415834
>>62410743
>NO ONE is trustworthy in crypto
No one is trustworthy. Cursed is the man who trusts in man.

62415841
Ver1zon hires the most pedophiles in the state of new jersey. Even with charges they keep their job touching kids that changes today.

62416152
>>62414317
True. As for Kucoin, I don't think they ever filed for chapter 11. They did cease operations in the US because I do remember them telling users to withdraw all of their holdings. Wasn't the fud around Tera Luna and Kucoin's supposed exposure to it? I think that was a rumor: https://www.kucoin.com/blog/be-aware-of-fud

62416320
>>62413262
>There are people in their telegram group talking about roping as a result of this. I'm not joking

62416840
Damn I trusted this exchange the most. It's so over.

62417095
>>62414281
But there ARE rules?

62418507
>>62416840
He really seemed like a solid guy with his weekly amas and willingness to follow regulations....and now this shit happens

62418517
>>62410760
he fucks

62418547
>>62418517
yeah camel herders in dugai

item.title: Nexo going bankrupt — 7 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nexo going bankrupt

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-27 00:59 UTC

OP

https://www.reddit.com/r/Nexo/comments/1ubmmp9/nexo_does_not_have_a_micacasp_license/

This ponzi exchange is finally going down. They don't even have a MiCA license, but they're pretending they do because they have some German license or whatever. If their MiCA application gets rejected, and it probably will, it's game over. There's no way this ponzi scheme actually has all of its users' funds backed and stored safely.
Bitcoin will got to 10000 dollars because like last time MICA will be the catalyst that bankrupts every exchange that doesn't have the funds to pay back all the europoors

Early replies (first 3)

62416186
>>62416072
i'm amazed they lasted this long

62417966
we're gonna make it bros

62417970
>>62416072
wow so bvllish

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417981
>>62416072
isnt this bullish?

paper BTC and other coins exchanges are using to influence price disappear?

62418595
>>62417981
Nah there’s not enough euros to bankrupt scamnance if they all withdrew their coins. Might have made them panic if this happened during the october flash crash after scamnance sold everyone’s coins into the ground.

62418630
who cares about mica? Just serve the euro niggers. what is Europe gonna do?

62418634
>>62416072
Shouldn't they be extremely wealthy just off their Chainlink shorts?

OP

Doge Army manifesting $1 DOGE OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62418650
OWIWOWOWOWOWOWOWOW

62418728
shib to 1 cent

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 164 replies — 47.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 164 • Updated: 2026-06-26 22:18 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 94 replies — 24.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-06-26 21:59 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

62412068
VOO bros are up bigly today

62413691
>>62412068
sweet

62413705
>>62400493
Felix and friends on youtube has a good way of putting it. Why invest in 500 companies when 50%+ are going down and sideways? If you have the time and money and a good strategy you can stick to you're better off picking stonks. But most people don't have the time, money and discipline to do so.

62413718
>>62413705
I think it's more like 20% of the s&p carries the other 80%, it is definitely lopsided. He (an ex investment banker) preaches buy and hold is a dead strategy.

62413734
>>62410053
Your "realism" doesn't sound realistic to people who actually do this stuff. It sounds retarded, like you've got no idea what you're actually talking about.

62413777
>>62413705
>>62413718
bc i cannot pick the winners, simple, look what happened today

62413963
If you don't just want to do VOO and chill only, just have a separate account at Vanguard that you treat like a savings account and deposit something there monthly to buy VOO or whatever. You'll be glad later on.

62414091
>>62412068
>checking your account daily
>investing only in one country

62414103
For me its 90% VOO
10% GPIQ
So i win even when I crab

62414142
>>62413705
>Felix and friends on youtube
>ex investment banker
>"Discover A Trading System That Makes Market Headlines Irrelevant"

62414162
>>62400493
Fuck VOO
>50% VT
>50% VTI
That's the real chill. Total U.S. investment is 81% of portfolio, you capture emerging markets, and some great companies you otherwise wouldn't have in VOO or VTI alone.

62414279
>>62414162
What’s the best way to compare the two? Track the CAGRs of VOO vs the average of VT and VTI combined

62414680
>>62414279
If you do that, its boomer tier analysis, otherwise that analysis would dictate to just buy VOO or QQQ overlooking the real value of VT/I. VT is used as a hedge if there's an exodus from U.S. markets in the next 5-10 years. VTI is used as a hedge against an exodus from bloated tech in the next 5-10 years but the U.S is still supreme (likely the case). When investment eventually settles from AI bloat and only 3-5 companies out of the current hundreds trying to pursue AI development come out on top, investment will switch into a different sector. With these etfs, you dont have to worry about what the next big thing is, which is why its the ultimate hold. Zero think. Comfy returns and they trail VOO by total return over 10 years 309% vs 325%, VT and VTI both have lowe p/e which makes the more value and less risk. Buy, hold, DRIP, and sell when you want to. This is the long end of my portfolio, and my 401k and IRAs. This is shit I am not touching for 30-40 years. Some people in my firm freak out over market turmoil, im zen, where one bucket empties, another fills for me.

62414768
>>62413705
>Felix and friends
pic is the better Felix

62414776
>>62414162
vti+vxus is more tax efficient

62414829
>>62414776
Barely, but VXUS does have the pas-through tax credit, something that amounts to $100 per $100k invested, which is better, plus you can set your parameters for how much you want allocated to international. But have you ever had to explain to a 24yo HR roastie to set up 15% portfolio allocation of VTIAX/VXUS?
>HALF HERE HALF THERE IT CANT BE THIS FUCKING HARD
The headache is not worth the hassle.

62415002
>>62410414
VOO is not 15%, its 10%
>since inception
I don't give a shit, its such a low sample that it's nearly statistically irrelevant. Here is that data from 1900-2016. US Large is VOO

62416552
Damn Breh just got a $2200 check do I just invest it boringly or do something fun with it

62417956
>>62416552
sweet

62418163
>>62414680
That's actually super comfy anon. I think I'll set up an account like that.

item.title: Another stablecoin just RUGGED — 35 replies — 18.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another stablecoin just RUGGED

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-06-26 23:51 UTC

OP

MIM (Magic Internet Money) stablecoin just rugged.
So many stablecoins are rugging right now it's crazy

Early replies (first 3)

62408562
IT has just begun

62408566
magic internet money still exists?

62408598
>>62408557
amazing they're even still around

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408875
>>62408804
no it isn't, this happened today

62409989
>>62408557
Who?

62410014
>>62408557
Wow so stable

62411316
would pay $100 to watch an interview between caleb hammer and the largest magic internet money bagholder

62411344
>>62408557
That's when you know the bear is getting serious and there will be major dumps until we hit the bottom.

62414655
>>62408557
Last time I farmed this was almost a year ago, the APY would frequently go to zero and they would have to fucking manually approve more emissions via multisig kek. The writing was on the wall for a long time

Now they've deleted the discord server after rugging everyone

62414658
>>62414655
I'm actually amazed they were still running this and never caught any legal flak for the role they played in the terraluna ponzi scheme and collapse

62414664
this one MIGHT be a slurper
hold on

62414718
>>62414664
The shit people will buy instead of Bitcoin. Just lmfao. Sad really.

62414728
so depending on the collat that they start hiking rates on... this might re-peg
might

62414746
most likely won't re-peg
>reason:
their depreciated cauldrons is where most of the dogshit collat likely is
even if liquidated, they might not cover for a full repeg

62414751
>>62414664
Why would I want to repay my debt?

62414752
>>62414655
>manually approve more emissions via multisig
LMAO

62414758
>>62414751
dudes who borrowed from this might be better off letting liquidation happen than getting their dogshit collat back
only God knows what fuckery might be in the "depreciated cauldrons"

62414805
>>62414664
I will not be buying any but if you buy and it repairs, sell it the second it does.
>>62414718
>Buying bitcoin
Lmfao Buy real coins with actual utility.

62414828
>>62414805
I won't buy, this shit has thin liquidity
people might panic and send it to fractions of a cent just for the fuck of it
of course, at that point everyone is going to crowd and buy
but right now, it just looks like something went structurally wrong
and by what that guy posted, considering this shit is managed by fucking multisigs that put rates manually into the contracts...
it kinda does

62414831
>>62414805
>if you buy and it repairs

How’s the weather in Bangladesh?

62414841
>>62414831
Reread what I wrote retard. I said I will not buy it but encouraged him to not baghold the exit repeg if it happens because it never recovers and stays. You are so cooked by doom scrolling reels it is insane. You are incapable of reading a single sentence with patience for comprehension.

62414846
>>62414828
Maybe it will have a bounce somewhere along the line like UST did but do not bag hold it hoping for complete par recovery. Look at the LUNC generals in the archive if you want to feel the blood drain from your face imagining being them (and link is being outperformed by fucking LUNC, if you want to REALLY imagine being a bagholder of the deepest kind lol).

62414884
Why are you retards still in crypto??

62415000
>>62408557
usdc and tether can also depeg, then it's truly over
they can kill crypto at any time they want, wouldn't take more than the epstein class seizing circle and tether or their reserves
their buddies would all sell first, but this is a card they can and will play eventually if the goyim get too powerful and you will lose everything

62416138
>>62414841
>>62414846
Nice double post, yeah it takes about two seconds of reading your gibberish (which no one else will ever reply to because you’re brown and your opinion doesn’t matter) to tell you’re ESL as fuck and should GTFO

62416716
>>62415000
>usdc and tether can also depeg
they can only market depeg
their collat is cash equivalent, literally impossible to depeg unless the US government ceases to exist

62416862
>>62414658
Did the people behind MIM play a role in that? I thought MIM was just used (among several other stablecoins) as an attack vector on Curve to de-peg UST. Which we later found out (through a lawsuit) was allegedly done by Jane Street.

Unless I'm missing something then blaming the MIM creators for it is the same as blaming the USDT and USDC creators, which makes no sense

62416965
>>62416862
This is the MIM marketcap. It spiked this high because of the UST "degenbox" strategy that took advantage of Terra's fixed high yields. See that first blip in January 2022? That was in reaction to the collapse of the Wonderland (TIME) ponzi scheme, which was run by the same people as MIM. This was the beginning of the end for Terra, as liquidity rapidly pulled out, Do Kwon would then make a fatal error when he failed to understand that billions of dollars could be withdrawn on a hair trigger. Is MIM solely responsible for Terraluna's collapse? No... but they were definitely in the thick of it

62417049
>>62416862
>Did the people behind MIM play a role in that?
daniele does not have the brains for that
he's mostly a vibes guy

62417050
>>62414664
>across all Cauldrons

lmao imagine buying this shit ever

62418339
>>62417050
Honestly I miss the playful themes of 2021, most every project nowadays takes themselves way too seriously

62418454
>>62418339
it's a fucking financial market
if you want funny names go play monopoly

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 89 replies — 17.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-06-26 20:40 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412797
>>62411160
go to work

62413082
>>62411638
That's the part most people miss. BTC doesn't have to just sit in a wallet forever.

62413193
>>62406783
YOU'RE RETARD

62414853
>>62409813
Good idea

62414893
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
because there is this one nigger saylormoon who hold nearly a million of these, only boughted with debts & baggies hopes and dreams. MSTR alone was most of the buying pressure the past 2 years. What would the price look like to your opinion if it wasn't for Mike buying more than all the BTC mined worldwide each year?
We would already be back in the 15k range if it wasn't for him throwing infinite monies at BTC. And now his preferential dividend model is failing, do you really believe MSTR will be able to continue the purchase frenzy?
In just 6 months STRC went from 7 years worth of dividends to pay in their treasury down to 13 months. This boat is bleeding cash left and right.

62414918
>>62409051
Stolen from Sam Hyde

62414938
>>62414893
He knows what's he's doing

62414950
>>62413082
It won't work forever

62414955
>>62414944
How?

62415214
>>62414950
Hmmm, that's why it's still in public testnet instead of being pushed straight into production. I'm more interested in where it could lead than writing it off this early.

62415217
>>62414893
MSTR has definitely been a major buyer, but I don't think Bitcoin's entire value proposition depends on one company continuing to buy forever.

62415228
>>62395655
Because I have no brown, black or asian admixture.

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

62417853
>>62415297
Memecoins are different

item.title: how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy? — 13 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-27 00:59 UTC

OP

how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Early replies (first 3)

62417182
know somebody

62417183
firm handshake

62417184
become a plumber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417247
be mexican / speak spanish

62417263
>>62417164
Be a brahmin

62417270
>>62417164
It’s so easy, you just spam indeed with the same format as they use on myperfectresume dot coom. 4 bullet points per job.

I’ve been working security for 4 years and I’ll do it until I die. Closest you can get to getting paid to do nothing

62417275
>>62417164
I’m honestly confused how you guys have so much trouble. I got laid off didn’t bother to look for a job for a couple of months and then got one after applying for like 3 weeks. Maybe it’s because I’m an accountant and people of my generation don’t want to do shit like that idk

62417357
pull yourself up by the bootstraps

62418447
learn to code welding robots

62418479
>>62417164
Pressure washer.
>>62417275
Same. I’m guessing it’s demoralizing bots.

62418486
Mind your fucking business. Do that for a few decades.

62418538
literally just get an entry level job at a big company like a bank and work your way up

I was a dropout neet in 2016 and I got a job at a mail room in a bank office part time making minimum wage. Made some moves up and now I'm a """"cyber security analyst"""" which is a bullshit laptop job.

62418632
>>62417275
ive never had a job in 10 years, i show up to interviews, awkwardly go through it, never hear back. I gave up desu. I have no idea how people get jobs or stay in them without burning out.

62414040

62414121

62414157

62414178
>>62408480

Merging xAI into SpaceX however just tied a 1 ton ball and chain around it, because it's an insatiable blackhole of capex with no clear upside.

62415681
>>62414137
he was spending too much time being a master of POE2 instead of going to the gym. he'll get back on the horse.

62415707

62415715
>>62415707
$150 is still the floor. it'll be $300 by hanukkah

62415763
>>62411812

62416005
>>62408480

Equivalent to buying Worldcomm with the exception that Space X probably has a longterm future, but is at absolute bubble top with an ABSURD valuation and no course to revenues in the near future, kept aloft by virtue of its multiple rules changed inclusion in indices receiving passive flows and retail hype that has come and gone.

item.title: Polkadot update — 37 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-26 22:23 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084
I'll believe they're serious when I see this on an fdroid repository

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700
>>62399197
Cope

62401739
>>62401689
Interesting
Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877
>>62401739
A lambo will be in your future

62401946
>>62401877
Checked.

62402307
>>62399251
You have a missed call

62402326
>>62402317
Didn't NEO have a similar issue

62402419
>>62402317
it's just an nft with price discovery so they don't have to do all of that coretime auction bullshit again

62403350
>>62402317

Thats not even what its use is for

62403448
>>62402317

im surprised dot isnt trading at 10 dollars considering the pedigree

but they overspent on marketing during a bear market instead of 2021. but on the plus side the dot treasury has started reporting quarterly profits. Unlike Cardano or Chainlink

it wont stay under a dollar for long

62404229
>>62403448
>it wont stay under a dollar for long

Under 50 cents by 2027

62404438
0.87 new low lmao. god what a piece of shit.

62404588
15k @ 7$.
I dont care

62404599
>>62404229
It'll go to 0. Nobody cares; it's a useless chain that serves to inflate Gayvin's ego, making him believe he's intelligent and respected. This desperate propaganda on /biz/ shows how dead this project is.

62405376
>>62401877
Checked
I've heard that before
We'll see
Been waiting for an actual parallel economy system for fucking ever
Was watching komodo and loki/oxen before this and they never got off the ground

62405404
Depression

62405433
Didn't read but is he hugging DOT and shilling a new token?

62406887
I bought more

62408015
>>62406887
Rookie move

62408521
>>62404588

If you buy 15k more your average will be 3.50

Why not DCA?

62408874
>>62404438

.885

62411243
wowie

62413316
>>62405433

JAM will not be a new token but will be an on chain platform with Polkadotbl

62417076
I want to lick Pokis feet

62418229
>>62399184
Polkadot JAM
>JAM is a newly maturing protocol
>to manifest a highly distributed collective computer.
So Polkadot JAM would function as a distributed computer.
One could for example save files in a distributed way!?
>The vast majority of the data
>that this computer stores and retrieves
>is in a subsystem known as its Data Lake
The other user explains JAMKB's function.

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 60 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:53 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

Early replies (first 3)

62401710
Wow I can’t wait for this to not mean anything.

62401722
p+c etc.
don't care until the needle moves on this FUCKING GODDAMN TOKEN

62401738

62409661

>>62406112

62409669

62409679

so how does it work in your head? 40 million altcoins should all have bitcoin or ethereum type price runup so things can become... fair?

you see folks, this is how the logic of a chainlink boyscout operates. no understanding that there isn't enough liquidity for their special altcoin to run like early crypto's did back when there was only like 50 of them. no understanding that obviously they aren't early anymore and therefore the days of those speculative runs are over.

instead of accepting the market they have, they cry like a child complaining to their parents that the other kids were allowed to have cookies before dinner.

>>62410035

>>62411677

62413787
>>62411795
